name: Psychedelic Treatments
description: Psilocybin, LSD, BOL-148, and other psychedelic compounds for cluster headache. The community's most-discussed treatment category, now backed by Phase 2 clinical trials.

all_trials.title: Psilocybin for the Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Oral psilocybin (low and high dose) given in 3 sessions spaced 5 days apart, to see if it reduces cluster headache frequency and intensity.
all_trials.key_result: Study completed in 2022 with 25 participants but no results have been posted yet. This was one of the first rigorous academic studies of psilocybin for CH.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Yale's landmark psilocybin study completed but awaits publication -- results could shape future psychedelic treatment options for CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: Low dose and high dose oral psilocybin (exact mg not specified), 3 sessions 5 days apart

all_trials.title: LSD to Improve Cluster Headache Impact Trial
all_trials.what_tested: Microdose LSD (25 micrograms) given every 3 days for 3 weeks to treat chronic cluster headache, compared to placebo.
all_trials.key_result: Currently recruiting 65 participants, expected to complete in 2027.
all_trials.patient_relevance: If successful, this could establish microdose LSD as a legitimate treatment for chronic CH -- a disease with very few good prevention options.
all_trials.dose_tested: 25 micrograms LSD every 3 days for 3 weeks

all_trials.title: Prophylactic Effects of Psilocybin on Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Low-dose psilocybin given in 3 weekly sessions as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache, with brain imaging (fMRI) before and after.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 10 of planned participants, likely due to COVID-19 pandemic (started Jan 2020, ended Nov 2020).
all_trials.patient_relevance: This Danish study was stopped early, probably due to COVID -- it does not tell us whether psilocybin works for chronic CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: Low dose psilocybin, 3 sessions one week apart

all_trials.title: Effect of Ketamine Intranasal Spray in Treatment of Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Intranasal ketamine spray (15mg per dose, up to 75mg total) as an acute rescue treatment for chronic cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 23 participants but no results posted. Based on the protocol, treatment was given during active attacks when pain exceeded 6/10.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A nasal ketamine spray could be a fast-acting rescue option for CH attacks, but we are still waiting for results from this small study.
all_trials.dose_tested: 15mg ketamine intranasal per dose, up to 5 doses (75mg total) over 24 minutes

all_trials.title: Lysergic Acid Diethylamide (LSD) as Treatment for Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Oral LSD (100 micrograms) given 3 times over 3 weeks versus placebo in a crossover design, to prevent cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Still recruiting 30 participants as of 2025, expected completion December 2025.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This Basel study tests a full psychoactive dose of LSD for CH prevention -- if it works, it could validate what many patients have reported anecdotally.
all_trials.dose_tested: 100 micrograms oral LSD, 3 doses over 3 weeks

all_trials.title: Ketamine + Magnesium for Chronic Cluster Headache (KETALGIA)
all_trials.what_tested: A single IV infusion of ketamine combined with magnesium to reduce attacks in drug-resistant chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Still recruiting 90 participants, expected completion September 2026. Earlier pilot data in 17 patients showed daily attacks dropped from 4.3 to 1.3 (p<0.001).
all_trials.patient_relevance: Promising pilot data suggests ketamine+magnesium IV could dramatically reduce attacks in treatment-resistant chronic CH -- this larger trial will tell us if it is real.
all_trials.dose_tested: Single IV infusion of ketamine-magnesium combination
